Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Ask HN: Apple terminated our dev account over a rogue employee 107 upvotes, 12 comments
  2. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 50 upvotes, 5 comments
  3. Palantir extends reach into British state as gets access to sensitive FCA data 31 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 30 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 257 upvotes, 50 comments
  6. Monuses and Heaps 40 upvotes, 4 comments
  7. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 116 upvotes, 20 comments
  8. My first patch to the Linux kernel 192 upvotes, 40 comments
  9. HopTab – Open source macOS app switcher and tiler that replaces Cmd+Tab 111 upvotes, 30 comments
  10. 25 Years of Eggs 200 upvotes, 61 comments
  11. A review of dice that came with the white castle 98 upvotes, 29 comments
  12. GrapheneOS refuses to comply with new age verification laws for operating system 46 upvotes, 11 comments
  13. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 234 upvotes, 88 comments
  14. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 66 upvotes, 21 comments
  15. Learnings from training a font recognition model from scratch 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 101 upvotes, 38 comments
  17. The Future of Version Control 203 upvotes, 113 comments
  18. Nebraska wildfires leave ranchers scrambling for forage 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. JavaScript Is Enough 52 upvotes, 25 comments
  20. Cloudflare flags archive.today as "C&C/Botnet"; no longer resolves via 1.1.1.2 304 upvotes, 230 comments
  21. Node.js worker threads are problematic, but they work great for us 49 upvotes, 29 comments
  22. Why Lab Coats Turned White 50 upvotes, 30 comments
  23. Vectorization of Verilog Designs and its Effects on Verification and Synthesis 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. OpenClaw Is a Security Nightmare Dressed Up as a Daydream 26 upvotes, 15 comments
  25. Why I love NixOS 84 upvotes, 69 comments
  26. Windows native app development is a mess 211 upvotes, 214 comments
  27. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 86 upvotes, 87 comments
  28. Show HN: Revise – An AI Editor for Documents 35 upvotes, 32 comments
  29. A case against currying 68 upvotes, 88 comments
  30. Zero ZGC4: A Better Graphing Calculator for School and Beyond 12 upvotes, 14 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 23 March 2026. Source here.
